title: Cache Expiration and TTL Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of cache expiration and data consistency in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is 'TTL' in the context of caching?
options: Total-Time-Limit
Time-To-Live; the duration for which a piece of data remains valid in the cache before it is automatically deleted.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: What's the primary risk of setting a TTL that's too long?
options: It makes the database slower.
The cache will serve stale, outdated data to users for an extended period.
It automatically generates a backup of the data.
It's the only way to store data in a database.

questions.question: Which Redis command option is used to set a TTL in seconds when saving a key?
options: EX
PX
LX
TX

questions.question: What is 'TTL Randomization' (Jitter) and why is it useful?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
Adding a small random offset to your TTLs to prevent large numbers of cache keys from expiring at the exact same time and overwhelming your database.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: When should you use 'Absolute' expiration instead of 'Relative' (sliding window)?
options: When you want a key to stay in the cache indefinitely.
When you absolutely must ensure that data is refreshed from the primary database after a specific period, regardless of how popular it is.
Only when the server is about to restart.
None of the above.
